response.justification: Bogdan is a capable senior software engineer with an MIT CS degree and broad experience across multiple languages, domains, and platforms spanning roughly 15 years, but he falls substantially short of this director-level AI strategy role. The position requires recognized AI expertise with demonstrated built products or platforms, enterprise-scale cloud fluency, and strategic customer advisory experience — none of which are clearly evidenced. His most recent AI exposure is infrastructure-adjacent (MCP tooling and agentic workflow frameworks at a small firm), not the deep technical AI strategy leadership the role demands, and there is no track record of advising enterprise customers, influencing organizational technical roadmaps, or delivering AI-powered platforms at scale.
strengths: MIT BS in Computer Science plus ~15 years of professional engineering experience across diverse stacks, domains, and system types
Current work on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq shows early, tangible alignment with the role's AI focus area
Founder and open-source contributor (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial) with conference talks and community workshops demonstrating entrepreneurial initiative and a willingness to share expertise
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML depth, no research background, and no recognized AI-powered products or platforms at scale — the primary differentiator this role is hiring for
No director-level or people leadership experience and no evidence of advising strategic enterprise customers or driving technical strategy at organizational scope

No enterprise cloud platform expertise; experience is concentrated in full-stack web and backend engineering without the scale, strategic influence, or cloud-native depth expected at principal/director level
